📜 Beschreibung 》
Spica Virgo träumt davon, an der angesehenen Royal Diana Academy zur Top-Magierin ausgebildet zu werden, so wie ihr verschwundenes Idol Claude Sirius. Doch ohne Ausbildung und Kontakte steckt sie fest, bis sie einer sprechenden schwarzen Katze mit magischen Kräften begegnet. Sie wird deren Schülerin unter einer Bedingung: Spica muss einen Weg finden, den Fluch zu brechen, der die Katze in ihre jetzige Gestalt gezwungen hat.

Der Opening-Song »Zettai MUST Danmen« und der Ending-Song »Return tu Atama Head« werden erneut von (K)NoW_NAME beigesteuert, der breits in Staffel 1 für beide Theme-Songs verantwortlich war. Zudem stellte die Verantwortlichen den japanischen Sprechercast offiziell vor. Staffel 2 startet am 1. April bei Netflix im Simulcast. ADN und Crunchyroll kündigten ebenfalls an die zweite Staffel im Simulcast zu zeigen.

Tech News Roundup: Nothing Headphones, ChatGPT, Vinted Scams & More | 01net The tech landscape is constantly shifting, and this week brings a mix of anticipated delays, innovative product relea...

#Tech

Origin | Interest | Match

external.description:
The tech landscape is constantly shifting, and this week brings a mix of anticipated delays, innovative product releases, and concerning security vulnerabilities. From the postponement of “adult mode” in ChatGPT to a novel wave of AI-powered fraud on Vinted, and the launch of Nothing’s latest audio offering, here’s a breakdown of the key developments impacting consumers and the industry.

The consumer electronics market continues to witness fierce competition, with companies striving to balance innovation and affordability. Simultaneously, the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence presents both exciting opportunities and new challenges, particularly in the realm of online security. This week’s headlines underscore the need for vigilance and informed decision-making in an increasingly complex digital world.

## Nothing Headphone (a) Offers Premium Features at a Mid-Range Price

Nothing, the London-based consumer tech company, is making waves with its new Headphone (a), a more accessible version of its popular Headphone (1). Priced at €159 (approximately $170 USD as of March 9, 2026), the Headphone (a) aims to deliver a premium audio experience without the premium price tag. According to a review by 01net, the headphones boast impressive battery life, a comfortable ergonomic design, and engaging sound quality. The review highlights that Nothing has successfully retained the core functionality of the Headphone (1) even as significantly reducing the cost.

The Headphone (a) features Bluetooth 5.4 connectivity, USB-C audio, and a mini-jack, offering versatile compatibility. It similarly includes an 8-band parametric equalizer for customizable sound profiles. While the active noise cancellation (ANC) isn’t as effective in the lower frequencies compared to top-tier competitors, and the included case is a simple soft pouch rather than a rigid shell, the overall package represents a compelling value proposition. The headphones weigh 310g and feature circum-aural earcups.

## ChatGPT’s “Adult Mode” Delayed Indefinitely

Users hoping for expanded capabilities in OpenAI’s ChatGPT will have to wait longer. The much-discussed “adult mode,” initially announced by CEO Sam Altman in October 2025, has been indefinitely postponed. Reports indicate that the feature, intended to allow for more explicit and nuanced conversations, is facing unforeseen challenges. OpenAI has not provided a new timeline for its release.

The delay raises questions about the complexities of implementing responsible AI safeguards for sensitive content. Balancing freedom of expression with the need to prevent misuse and harmful outputs remains a significant hurdle for developers of large language models like ChatGPT. The initial announcement sparked considerable debate about the ethical implications of such a feature, and OpenAI appears to be taking a cautious approach.

## AI-Generated Images Fuel New Vinted Scam

The popular resale platform Vinted is grappling with a novel form of fraud leveraging the power of artificial intelligence. Scammers are utilizing AI image generators to fabricate evidence of damaged goods, falsely claiming items arrived in poor condition to secure fraudulent refunds. This new tactic poses a significant threat to sellers on the platform, who are bearing the financial burden of these deceptive claims.

The sophistication of AI image generation makes it increasingly difficult to distinguish between genuine damage and fabricated evidence. Vinted is reportedly investigating the issue and implementing measures to detect and prevent these fraudulent activities. This incident highlights the growing need for platforms to adapt to the evolving tactics of online scammers and invest in AI-powered fraud detection systems. Users are advised to carefully document the condition of items before shipping and to report any suspicious activity to Vinted’s support team.

## Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra: Early Autonomy Tests

Ahead of its official release and full review, Samsung’s upcoming Galaxy S26 Ultra has undergone preliminary testing by 01Lab, focusing specifically on battery life. The tests, conducted using a new proprietary protocol, aim to provide an early indication of the device’s performance compared to its predecessor, the Galaxy S25 Ultra. The initial results suggest potential improvements in autonomy, though a comprehensive assessment will require a full review of the device.

The Galaxy S26 Ultra is expected to be a flagship device, boasting cutting-edge technology and features. Battery life is a critical factor for smartphone users, and Samsung’s efforts to enhance autonomy are likely to be a key selling point for the new model. Further details regarding the S26 Ultra’s specifications and performance will be revealed as the launch date approaches.

## Sophisticated iPhone Hacking Tool Linked to Government Agencies

Google has uncovered a highly sophisticated hacking kit capable of compromising iPhones. The tool, reportedly exploited in cyberattacks targeting Ukraine and China, is believed to have been developed for intelligence gathering purposes, potentially by a government agency – with some reports suggesting US government involvement. The discovery raises serious concerns about the proliferation of advanced hacking tools and the potential for misuse.

The hacking kit’s capabilities are described as exceptionally advanced, allowing attackers to gain deep access to targeted devices. The alleged involvement of a government agency adds a layer of complexity to the situation, raising questions about the ethical and legal implications of such activities. This incident underscores the ongoing need for robust cybersecurity measures and international cooperation to combat cyber threats.

## Nintendo Seeks Reimbursement for Trump-Era Tariffs

Nintendo is joining a growing number of companies seeking reimbursement for tariffs imposed during the Trump administration. Following a recent defeat in the Supreme Court regarding the legality of these tariffs, Nintendo is pursuing a claim to recover funds paid on imported goods. The company argues that it ultimately bore the cost of these tariffs, which were levied on products imported from China.

The Supreme Court ruling opened the door for companies to seek refunds for tariffs paid, and Nintendo is among the first to accept action. The outcome of these claims could have significant financial implications for both companies and the US government. This situation highlights the complex economic consequences of trade policies and the importance of legal challenges to ensure fairness and transparency.

Motorola recently unveiled a new device designed to simplify the use of Android Auto wirelessly. The compact box allows users to connect their Android smartphones to their car’s infotainment system without the need for cables, offering a more convenient and streamlined experience.

Looking ahead, the tech industry will continue to navigate a landscape of rapid innovation and evolving challenges. The ongoing development of AI, the increasing sophistication of cyber threats, and the complexities of global trade will all shape the future of technology. The next major checkpoint will be the full release of Samsung’s Galaxy S26 Ultra, expected in the coming months, providing a more comprehensive assessment of its capabilities.

What are your thoughts on these developments? Share your comments below and let us know how these trends are impacting you.

external.title: If I could go on a luxury shopping spree, I would travel to Dubai Mall, one of the biggest and most luxurious shopping destinations in the world.
